meta.topic: Forming passive sentences: verb + -ing or to-infinitive

step_1_context.text: Verbs followed by object + -ing in the active are made passive with 'be' + past participle + -ing:

step_2_explanation.simple_rule:
Verbs followed by object + -ing in the active are made passive with 'be' + past participle + -ing:
 • They saw the monkey climbing over the fence.
step_2_explanation.formula:
Some verbs that can be followed by an -ing form can be used with a passive form being + past participle:
 • I really love being given presents.
step_2_explanation.common_mistake:
Verbs which in the active are followed by an object consisting of a noun phrase and -ing clause usually have no passive:
 • I dread him (or his) finding out.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 24.1.1 Inger ___ for over three hours when she went for her dental appointment. (keep - wait)
24.1.2 When the police first questioned him, Wayne ___ in the robbery. (deny - involve)
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Passive form of -ing form: being + past participle.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 24.1.3 I ___ the baby while Karen went to answer the door. (observe - hide)
24.1.4 When I woke up in hospital, I ___ by the snake but nothing after that. (remember - bite)
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Passive form of -ing: being + past participle.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 24.1.6 The man ___ a suspicious package under a seat in the train. (find - wander)
